description_long: This object is a classic submachine gun with a robust, elongated black metal receiver and barrel, complemented by dark brown wooden furniture. The stock is a solid, fixed wooden piece, providing stability and a traditional aesthetic. The foregrip is uniquely contoured with finger grooves, also made of wood, enhancing grip and control. The magazine is a straight, detachable box type, positioned vertically beneath the receiver. Iron sights are mounted along the top of the receiver and barrel, and the muzzle is simple, lacking any additional device. The overall design is reminiscent of early to mid-20th century firearms, combining utilitarian metalwork with ergonomic wooden components.
description: A classic submachine gun with a black metal body, brown wooden stock and foregrip, and a straight box magazine. Features iron sights and a simple, unadorned muzzle.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length, wooden stock, contoured foregrip, and straight box magazine.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the round muzzle, iron front sight, and the narrow profile of the barrel and foregrip.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the wooden stock, foregrip, and magazine, mirroring the details of the first side.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the end of the wooden stock and the alignment of the receiver and barrel.
